title: Paired-side VESC liveness (CAN drop detection)
description: If right VESC (CAN ID 2) fails N consecutive reads, send synthetic TR telemetry with fault=99 + stop motors. Drive() refuses to send commands when _can_lost. Prevents one-wheel runaway.

title: Slave boot banner + Master VESC config resync
description: Slave sends BOOT:READY:CRC after uart.start(). Master registers BOOT callback that re-pushes VCFG:scale, VINV:L, VINV:R. Prevents config drift after Slave reboot.

title: Universal VESC safety: shared helper + bench mode consistency
description: Create master/vesc_safety.py as single source of truth. Update motion_bp, status_bp, bt_controller_driver, choreo_player to all use it. None telem must block drive when bench_mode=False.

title: script_engine.py: circular .scr includes cause infinite recursion — crash
description: master/script_engine.py _run_file() calls itself recursively when a script includes another script via the 'script,name' command. There is no depth counter or cycle detection. A .scr file that directly or indirectly includes itself (A→B→A) will recurse until Python's default stack limit (~1000 frames) is hit, raising RecursionError that crashes the script engine thread. A malicious or accidentally recursive .scr file can take down the Master service. Noted in a comment in the code ('WARNING: recursive') but no protection.
notes: Files: master/script_engine.py _run_file() around line 486. Fix: add a depth parameter defaulting to 0, raise ValueError if depth > MAX_DEPTH (e.g. 10), and pass depth+1 on recursive calls. Or maintain a set of currently-executing script paths and reject re-entry.

title: vesc_can.py: no bounds check before struct.unpack — crash on short VESC packet
description: slave/drivers/vesc_can.py parses VESC telemetry packets using sequential struct.unpack_from() calls with an advancing offset p. There is no check that p + sizeof(field) <= len(payload) before each unpack. If the VESC sends a truncated packet (firmware glitch, CAN noise, partial USB read), unpack_from reads past the buffer end, returning garbage values that get stored as telemetry (temp, current, RPM) — or raises struct.error which propagates uncaught to the telemetry thread and kills it silently. With a dead telemetry thread, vesc_driver stops sending TL:/TR: to the Master, which counts as UART failures and can abort a running choreo.
notes: Files: slave/drivers/vesc_can.py get_values_direct() and get_values_can(). Fix: validate len(payload) >= EXPECTED_MIN_LEN (should be ~54 bytes for full GET_VALUES response) before parsing, and return None immediately on short packet. Add the check once at the top of each function.
close_reason: False positive: get_values_direct() and get_values_can() both already check len(payload) < 54 at lines 188 and 246 before any struct.unpack_from calls. All paths also have try/except Exception.

title: Service worker cache versioned with deploy commit
description: Replace static astromech-v1 cache name with current VERSION (git rev-parse short SHA). Serve sw.js dynamically via Flask route.

title: Lock /choreo/play to serialize concurrent requests
description: Module-level Lock around the stop→reset→play sequence in choreo_bp.choreo_play().

title: settings_bp config write lock
description: _write_key / _sync_slave_hat_cfg protected by threading.Lock to prevent concurrent RMW from losing keys.

title: ChoreoPlayer loop-mode full state reset + spin guard
description: On loop boundary: release audio slots, reset _drive_fail_count, _servo_pos, re-read arm_panel_map. Force min TICK wait when duration < TICK.

title: ChoreoPlayer Timer/slew respect _stop_flag
description: Track arm Timers in self._arm_timers, cancel on stop. Slew threads check _stop_flag between steps.

title: Skip /heartbeat in last_activity tracker
description: flask_app.py:88 _update_last_activity fires on every POST including /heartbeat (200ms). Excludes /heartbeat path so inactivity timer reflects real user activity.

title: Fix LOCAL_CFG undefined NameError in status_bp.estop_reset
description: cfg.read(LOCAL_CFG) at line 451 — should be _LOCAL_CFG. Causes silent NameError in safe-home thread.

title: Fix vesc telemetry key mismatch curr/current in status_bp
description: status_bp.py:259-260 reads .get('curr') but parser stores 'current'. UI always shows null current. Rename to 'current'.

title: Backlog: VESC safety lock si VESC absent/fault
description: La détection de fault VESC existe déjà (vesc_bp.py telemetry, app.js classes vesc-fault/fault-active). Ce qui manque: (1) overlay Drive tab qui bloque la propulsion si VESC L ou R est absent ou en fault, (2) désactivation du joystick web/BT/Android pendant le fault. Voir project_vesc_safety_lock.md dans bd memories.

title: servo_bp: SRV:RELOAD sent to Slave even when SCP failed — Slave reloads stale data
description: Introduced in commit ac52547 (software-fmf fix). In master/api/servo_bp.py _sync_angles_json(), reg.uart.send('SRV','RELOAD') is called unconditionally after the SCP subprocess.run() call, regardless of SCP's return code (check=False). If the Slave Pi is unreachable (WiFi drop, reboot), the new servo_angles.json was never transferred, but the Slave receives RELOAD and reloads its existing (stale) JSON, believing it now has the updated calibration. The user thinks calibration was saved but the Slave is still using old angles.
notes: Files: master/api/servo_bp.py _sync_angles_json() lines 183-190. Fix: check scp result.returncode == 0 before sending RELOAD. Also log a clear warning if SCP fails so the user knows the Slave was not updated.

title: vesc_driver.py: right VESC failure not counted — dead right motor goes undetected
description: slave/drivers/vesc_driver.py telemetry loop: _fail_count is only incremented when get_values_direct() (left/VESC1 USB) returns None. If get_values_can() (right/VESC2 CAN) returns None but get_values_direct() succeeds, TL: is sent but TR: is silently dropped and _fail_count is not incremented. A completely dead right motor/CAN bus causes 0 logged failures and no choreo abort, while the robot drives in circles with one motor. The ChoreoPlayer 3-failure abort threshold never triggers for right-side failures.
notes: Files: slave/drivers/vesc_driver.py _telemetry_loop() around line 385. Fix: only reset _fail_count if BOTH vl and vr are non-None; increment _fail_count if either is None. Or track L/R fail counts independently.

title: settings_bp: _reload_lights_driver sets reg.teeces=None before new driver ready — other threads crash
description: master/api/settings_bp.py _reload_lights_driver() sets reg.teeces = None as a guard during the driver swap. If the new driver's setup() returns False or raises, reg.teeces stays None permanently. Any concurrent request to /teeces/* or any behavior engine tick that calls reg.teeces.random() will crash with AttributeError: 'NoneType' has no attribute 'random'. The old (working) driver is also lost with no fallback path.
notes: Files: master/api/settings_bp.py _reload_lights_driver() lines ~113-128. Fix: keep old driver in a local variable, only replace reg.teeces on successful setup, restore old driver on failure: old = reg.teeces; reg.teeces = new if new.setup() else old.

title: App heartbeat respects document visibility
description: Stop heartbeat interval when tab hidden (visibilitychange). Background tabs no longer fight setInterval throttling. Drive cut on hidden = correct safety behavior, now consistent.

title: StatusPoller in-flight check
description: poll() guards on this._inFlight = true. setInterval can't pile up requests when Pi is slow.

title: UART buffer overflow keeps trailing 256B
description: Instead of resetting buffer to '' on overflow, keep last 256 bytes — the start of an in-flight frame is preserved instead of dropping ~350ms of data silently.

title: UART track HB ACK age + expose via /status
description: UARTController records _last_hb_ack_t on every H:OK reply. /status exposes slave_hb_age_ms so UI can distinguish 'Slave dead' from 'Slave alive but TX fried'.

title: Audio startup latency config (mpg123 cold-start)
description: Add audio_startup_lat (default 50ms). Audio events get t -= audio_startup_lat to compensate for mpg123 spawn delay so audio aligns with shifted lights/dome/body events.

title: ChoreoPlayer event-driven scheduler (no fixed TICK)
description: Replace _stop_flag.wait(TICK) with wait(min(TICK, next_event_dt)). Keeps TICK as upper bound for status updates but events fire within ~1ms instead of average 25ms (half-tick).

title: Feat: E-STOP red pulsing border overlay
description: Full-screen red pulsing border overlay (#estop-overlay) when E-STOP is tripped. pointer-events:none so it doesn't block interactions. Synced from data.estop_active on every status poll — survives page reload.

title: Feat: topbar cleanup — pills moved to cockpit, SLAVE pill added
description: Removed HB/UART/BT/Version pills and uptime from topbar. Added SLAVE conditional pill (visible when slave offline). Added HB/UART/BT pills row inside cockpit panel. BT pill shows orange when RSSI <= -75 dBm.

title: Fix: cockpit STATUS button color synced on every poll
description: STATUS button was only updating color when cockpit panel was open. Fixed by calling cockpitPanel.updateBtn(data) on every status poll.

title: Refactor: replace hardcoded /home/artoo/r2d2 paths with dynamic BASE_DIR
description: 33 Python files and 33 shell scripts hardcode /home/artoo/r2d2. The future installer will let users choose their username and install path. All paths need to be derived dynamically (Path(__file__).parents[N] or BASE_DIR from env/config) so the system works for any username and any install location.

title: Backup & Restore — full config + choreo export/import
description:
Allow builders to export all user-specific data to a single zip file and restore it after SD card replacement or new install.

## Why
SD cards corrupt. Without backup, a builder loses all their servo calibration, choreographies, and settings. This feature is the safety net — paired with the SD image installer, a full recovery takes minutes.

## Backup content (single .zip)
- master/config/local.cfg
- slave/config/slave.cfg
- master/config/dome_angles.json
- slave/config/servo_angles.json
- master/choreographies/*.chor (all custom sequences)
- master/config/choreo_categories.json
- master/config/bt_config.json (bluetooth gamepad mapping)

## UI
- Settings → System: 'Download Backup' button → downloads astromechos_backup_YYYY-MM-DD.zip to browser
- Settings → System: 'Import Backup' button → file picker → uploads zip → extracts to correct paths on Master + SCP to Slave → service reload

## API
- GET  /system/backup        → streams zip file download
- POST /system/restore       → multipart upload zip → extract + SCP to Slave + reload angles

## Restore flow
1. Builder flashes new SD with installer (config pre-filled from wizard OR blank)
2. Opens dashboard → Settings → System → Import Backup
3. Selects their .zip from phone/PC
4. System extracts: local.cfg, slave.cfg, angles, choreos, categories, bt_config
5. SCPs slave files to Slave Pi
6. Reloads servo drivers (angles) and choreo list
7. Toast: 'Backup restored — X choreos, servo angles reloaded'

## Notes
- Backup zip should include a manifest.json with version + date for validation
- On restore: validate manifest before extracting (wrong format = clear error)
- local.cfg restore should NOT overwrite network settings already working (hotspot/wifi)
  OR offer a choice: 'Restore all' vs 'Restore config only (keep network settings)'
- This feature integrates with the SD card installer (software-beg)

title: Bugfix: config writes atomiques + audio Popen + servo reload
description: 3 bugs documentés: (1) Écritures config.ini non-atomiques → corruption si crash pendant save. (2) audio Popen bloque le thread watchdog UART. (3) angles servo non-rechargés après save dans Settings — il faut reload depuis fichier sans restart. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-29-bugfix-config-audio-servo.md

title: Cockpit Status Panel — dashboard header overlay
description: Panneau STATUS collapsible dans le header du dashboard web. Montre tous les vitaux du robot depuis n'importe quel onglet sans changer de tab: batterie, températures VESC L/R, temp Pi, uptime, état services (UART/VESC/BT/caméra/servos), activité choreo/audio/ALIVE, alertes critiques. Plan détaillé: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-30-cockpit-status-panel.md

title: Mobile HUD: révision complète de l'interface Phone
description: L'interface mobile (mobile.html/mobile.js/mobile.css) a été développée rapidement et nécessite une révision complète. Fonctionnalités actuelles: joysticks propulsion+dome, drawer Audio/SEQ/Lights, VESC safety lock, batterie, E-STOP. La révision devrait revoir l'ergonomie, la disposition, l'UX tactile et s'assurer que tout est cohérent avec l'usage en convention/démonstration.

title: AstroPixels+: exposer 23+ animations via @APLE dans le driver
description:
CORRECTION: @APLE n'existe pas — l'analyse précédente était incorrecte.

RÉALITÉ (vérifiée dans MarcduinoLogics.h):
Via série, seulement 7 T-codes définis: @0T1(Normal) @0T2(Flash) @0T3(Alarm) @0T4(Failure) @0T5(RedAlert) @0T6(Leia) @0T11(March). RAINBOW, FIRE, etc. pas accessibles via série actuellement.

Les 23 animations existent dans le firmware (logic-sequences.h confirmé) mais seulement via web UI de la board ou via modification firmware.

OPTION 1 — Modifier firmware (recommandé):
Ajouter des MARCDUINO_ACTION dans MarcduinoLogics.h pour les animations manquantes:
  @0T10 → RAINBOW, @0T22 → FIRE, @0T9 → COLORSWAP, etc.
Puis reflasher via OTA (web update intégré).

OPTION 2 — WiFi HTTP:
Si WiFi activé sur la board, POST à http://[board-ip]/logics avec frontseq=N&run=1
Accessible via requests depuis le Master Pi.

Firmware source: J:/R2-D2_Build/software/others/AstroPixelsPlus-main/MarcduinoLogics.h

title: Android: révision et évolution de l'interface mobile
description: L'app Android (WebView) n'a pas été touchée depuis un moment. Revoir l'interface mobile.html/mobile.js : qualité du HUD actuel, joysticks (JoyRing déjà présent), ergonomie sur tablette/téléphone, fonctionnalités manquantes vs dashboard web. Identifier les améliorations prioritaires avant d'envisager un vrai HUD natif (software-bj8). Points à évaluer: layout portrait/paysage, taille des contrôles, feedback visuel, latence, ce qui manque sur mobile vs web.

title: Android: Thread leaks in ping loop and auto-discover — no lifecycle binding
description: MainActivity.kt spawns bare Thread{} in startPingLoop() (~line 137), startAutoDiscover() (~line 173), and discovery dialog (~line 372). These threads are not bound to the Activity lifecycle. On Activity destruction (rotation, back press), threads continue running and call runOnUiThread() on a potentially dead context. Should use lifecycleScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) or check isDestroyed before runOnUiThread calls. Risk: NullPointerException in edge cases (activity destroyed during network call).

title: motion_bp.py: arcade() endpoint has no UART fallback — silent failure when VESC unavailable
description: master/api/motion_bp.py arcade() endpoint calls reg.vesc.arcade_drive() directly. Unlike the /motion/drive endpoint which falls back to reg.uart.send('M',...) when VESC is not ready, arcade() returns 200 OK with no error and no UART fallback. During bench testing without VESCs connected (common during dev), arcade commands from the Android joystick appear to work (no error) but nothing moves. This is inconsistent with the rest of the motion API and can mask connectivity issues.
notes: Files: master/api/motion_bp.py arcade() endpoint. Fix: add elif reg.uart: reg.uart.send('M', f'{left:.3f},{right:.3f}') fallback after the vesc path, computing differential drive from speed+steering inputs.

title: bt_controller_driver.py: ecodes.BTN.get() can return None — unhandled TypeError crash
description: master/drivers/bt_controller_driver.py _handle_button(): the code calls ecodes.BTN.get(event.code) and then checks 'isinstance(raw, (list,tuple))' to handle multi-alias codes. However if event.code is not in BTN (unmapped button on a non-standard gamepad, or a spurious event), get() returns None. Then list(None) or raw[0] raises TypeError which propagates uncaught to the evdev poll loop and kills the BT controller thread — disconnecting the gamepad until the next reconnect cycle (5s). Reproducible with any controller that sends non-standard button codes (e.g. touchpad click on DualShock).
notes: Files: master/drivers/bt_controller_driver.py _handle_button() around line 505. Fix: add 'if raw is None: return' immediately after the get() call, before any isinstance check.
close_reason: False positive: _handle_button() already has 'if not raw: return' guard at line 505 immediately after the BTN.get() call, before any list/index operations.

title: slave/main.py: NameError on shutdown if servo or vesc driver init raises exception
description: slave/main.py shutdown sequence (around lines 285-310) calls vesc.shutdown() and servo.shutdown() without guarding against the case where setup raised an exception and the variable was never assigned. If servo.setup() raises (not just returns False) before servo is assigned, the shutdown block hits 'if servo.is_ready(): servo.shutdown()' and raises NameError: name 'servo' is not defined — preventing clean shutdown of all other drivers. Same risk for vesc, display, audio.
notes: Files: slave/main.py driver init and shutdown blocks. Fix: initialize all driver variables to None before their setup() calls (vesc = None, servo = None, etc.) so shutdown guard 'if servo and servo.is_ready()' is safe.

title: safe_stop.py: cancel event cleared before ramp thread starts — rapid commands cause abrupt stop
description: master/safe_stop.py _ramp_to_stop(): _cancel_drive Event is cleared (line ~96) before the ramp thread is started (line ~120). If another thread calls cancel_ramp() in that window — e.g. a new drive command arrives — the event is set before the thread starts. The new thread checks the event immediately, sees it set, and exits without ramping. Motors stop abruptly (0,0 sent directly) instead of the intended 400ms gradual deceleration. Most visible when joystick releases and a new command arrives within ~1ms.
notes: Files: master/safe_stop.py around lines 86-120. Fix: clear the event inside the new thread at its start (before the ramp loop), not before thread creation.
close_reason: False positive: cancel_ramp() in motion_bp.drive() is always immediately followed by the new drive command. The ramp thread exiting due to _cancel_drive means the new drive values take over — no abrupt stop occurs.

title: watchdog.py: elapsed calculated inside lock but checked outside — false VESC cutoff possible
description: slave/watchdog.py _watch_loop(): elapsed and already_triggered are read under self._lock (lines 89-92), but the if-condition that triggers VESC cutoff is evaluated OUTSIDE the lock (line 93). Between the lock release and the if-check, watchdog.feed() can run in another thread: it resets _last_heartbeat and clears _triggered. The main thread then evaluates the stale elapsed (which was >500ms), fires the cutoff, and calls emergency_stop_vesc() even though a fresh heartbeat just arrived. Result: VESCs cut during normal operation if a heartbeat and the watchdog check coincide within a few microseconds.
notes: Files: slave/watchdog.py _watch_loop() lines 86-104. Fix: move the if-condition inside the lock, or re-read _triggered inside the if block to confirm it's still False before firing.

title: Cache local.cfg reads in /status (TTL 2s)
description: status_bp re-parses local.cfg 5x per /status call (_robot_name/_icon/_location/_battery_cells/_slave_host). TTL cache + invalidation on settings save = ~30ms saved per poll.

title: SD card image installer / first-boot setup wizard
description:
Create an AstromechOS installer in the style of Home Assistant OS / RetroPie.

## Vision

Builder experience:
1. Download AstromechOS Installer (Windows/Mac/Linux desktop app)
2. Fill in the setup wizard (all parameters in one place)
3. Click 'Write Master SD' then 'Write Slave SD'
4. Insert cards, power on → robot is operational immediately

## Components

### 1. Pre-built base images (built once by dev, hosted on GitHub Releases)
- master-base.img.xz — Raspberry Pi OS + AstromechOS full code + all dependencies pre-installed
  (flask, paramiko, smbus2, adafruit-pca9685, python3-evdev, mpg123, etc.)
  + systemd services configured (r2d2-master, camera, etc.)
- slave-base.img.xz — same for Slave Pi (r2d2-slave service, audio, RP2040, etc.)
- Images are versioned and hosted on GitHub Releases
- Updated with each major release

### 2. Desktop installer app (runs on builder's PC)
- Cross-platform (Windows primary, Mac/Linux bonus)
- Downloads latest base images from GitHub Releases (or uses local file)
- Setup wizard collects all parameters:
  - WiFi hotspot SSID + password
  - Home WiFi SSID + password (for git auto-pull)
  - GitHub repo URL + branch
  - Admin password
  - Battery cell count (4S/6S/7S/8S)
  - Master servo HAT address(es) — master_hats
  - Slave motor HAT address — slave_motor_hat (shown as info/warning)
  - Slave servo HAT address(es) — slave_hats (validated: must not match motor HAT)
  - Number of arms (0-4)
  - Startup behavior on/off
- Generates local.cfg (Master) and slave.cfg (Slave) from wizard answers
- Injects generated configs into the base image (mount image, write files)
- Flashes to SD card (uses dd / Win32DiskImager API / custom)
- Two separate write buttons: 'Write Master SD' and 'Write Slave SD'

### 3. First boot behavior
- Pi boots → services start → robot operational immediately (zero Pi-side setup)
- If wlan1 (home WiFi) available: auto_pull_on_boot = true checks git for newer version
  → pulls update if available, restarts services
- This mechanism already exists in local.cfg + update.sh

## Tech stack options for the installer app
- Python + tkinter or wxPython (simple, cross-platform, same language as the rest)
- Electron (nicer UI, heavier)
- Python + customtkinter (modern look, lightweight)
Recommendation: Python + customtkinter — consistent with project language, modern UI, pip-installable

## Notes
- Base image build process should be scripted (scripts/build_image.sh) for reproducibility
- Image versioning matches git tags
- Installer validates SD card size before writing (Master needs ~8GB min)
- Both images share the same wizard answers where applicable (WiFi, hotspot, etc.)

title: Multi-HAT servo detection — dynamic Calibration + Choreo blocks
description:
Support additional PCA9685 servo HATs on Master (dome) and Slave (body). The Calibration menu and Choreo timeline adapt dynamically based on which HATs are configured.

## Current hardware (ELECTRONICS.md)
Master: Waveshare Servo HAT (PCA9685 @ 0x40) → Servo_M0-M15 (11 used for dome panels)
Slave:  Waveshare Motor HAT (TB6612 @ 0x40) → dome rotation motor — NOT a servo HAT
Slave:  PCA9685 Breakout (@ 0x41) → Servo_S0-S15 (11 used for body panels)

## WARNING: no blind I2C scan
The Waveshare Motor HAT at 0x40 on the Slave likely uses a PCA9685 internally for PWM.
A blind I2C scan would find TWO PCA9685s on the Slave (0x40 motor + 0x41 servo) and
confuse them. Detection MUST be address-list based, not auto-discovery.

## Implementation approach
- local.cfg [i2c_servo_hats] lists explicit servo HAT addresses per Pi:
    master_hats = 0x40          (default — Dome Servos 1)
    slave_hats  = 0x41          (default — Body Servos 1)
  User adds extra addresses when they physically install a new HAT.

- Servo ID naming (Option A — continue numbering):
    Master HAT 1 (0x40): Servo_M0–M15
    Master HAT 2 (0x42): Servo_M16–M31
    Slave  HAT 1 (0x41): Servo_S0–S15
    Slave  HAT 2 (0x42): Servo_S16–S31

## Calibration UI
- 'Dome Panels' section → renamed 'Dome Servos 1'
- If master_hats has a second address → 'Dome Servos 2' block appears below
- 'Body Panels' section → renamed 'Body Servos 1'
- If slave_hats has a second address → 'Body Servos 2' block appears below
- Each block shows 16 channels with label/open/close/speed fields
- Labels auto-update when Arms config assigns a servo as Arm slot

## Choreo timeline
- body_servos track: shows S0-S31 (or however many HATs configured)
- dome_servos track: shows M0-M31 (or however many HATs configured)
- Arms track: unchanged (uses abstract Arm1/Arm2 slots, not servo IDs)

## Driver changes
- dome_servo_driver.py: instantiate one DomeServoDriver per address in master_hats
- body_servo_driver.py (Slave): instantiate one BodyServoDriver per address in slave_hats
- Channels 0-15 = HAT 1, channels 16-31 = HAT 2 (driver maps index to correct HAT)

title: SD card image creator — first-run setup wizard
description:
Create an installer that builds a ready-to-flash SD card image for Master + Slave Pi. Inspired by Raspberry Pi Imager's OS customisation wizard — ask all install-specific questions up front so the image boots fully configured with no manual editing.

## Phase 1 — OS level (mirrors Pi Imager)
- Master hostname (default: r2-master)
- Slave hostname (default: r2-slave)
- Pi username (default: artoo)
- Pi password (default: deetoo)
- Timezone + keyboard layout + WiFi country/region
- Home WiFi SSID + password (wlan1 — internet/git pull)
- SSH enabled: yes by default (required for deployment)
- SSH auth method: password or public key

## Phase 2 — AstromechOS config (writes local.cfg on both images)
- Robot name shown in dashboard header (default: R2-D2)
- Hotspot SSID + password (wlan0 — controller connects here, default: R2D2_Control / r2d2droid)
- Hotspot IP for Master (default: 192.168.4.1)
- Admin dashboard password (default: deetoo)
- GitHub repo URL + branch (default: AstromechOS / main)
- Lights backend: teeces or astropixels
- Battery cell count: 4S / 6S / 7S / 8S
- VESC power scale (default: 1.0)
- Number of arms 0-4 (default: 0)

## Deferred to dashboard post-assembly
- Arm servo assignments, panel servos
- Startup choreo, ALIVE idle mode
- Dome button GPIO pin
- Servo calibration angles

## Output
- Pre-filled local.cfg on Master image
- Pre-filled local.cfg on Slave image
- SSH keys provisioned if chosen
- Ready to flash both SD cards

title: Caméra permanente: suivi personne AI (OpenCV + tracking)
description: Phase 5: intégrer suivi de personne sur le flux caméra (OpenCV). Caméra permanente OTG UVC 720/1080P commandée — hardware prêt dès réception. Traitement sur Master Pi4B (Vosk/Whisper.cpp + OpenCV). Contrôle dome automatique selon position personne dans le frame.

title: Lights plugin: backend teeces vs astropixels configurable
description: Refactoriser le système lights en plugin architecture: local.cfg [lights] backend = teeces|astropixels. UI dans Settings pour switcher et hot-reload sans redémarrer le Pi. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-25-lights-plugin-architecture.md

title: AstromechOS Image Builder — customized SD card images
description: A desktop/web wizard that collects all config (robot name, passwords, WiFi, hotspot SSID, Git repo URL) and produces two ready-to-flash .img files for Master and Slave. User flashes SD cards, connects UART cable, boots — robot is operational. Only servo calibration remains. Wizard also lets user point to their own Git fork for OTA updates. Avoids all manual SSH setup. Could use pi-gen or a first-boot config injection approach (simpler: write a config JSON to the SD card, first-boot script reads and applies it).

title: UI theme switcher — choose color theme from Settings
description: Add a theme selector in Settings that changes the color scheme of the entire web interface. The current dark blue/cyan palette would become the default theme. Additional themes could include: classic dark (green terminal), warm amber, imperial red, etc. Implementation: CSS custom properties (--blue, --accent, --bg, etc.) are already used everywhere — swapping a theme class on <body> or <html> would be enough. Theme choice persisted in localStorage (no server round-trip needed). Selector UI in Settings → Appearance or Settings → General panel.

title: Add Admin lock/unlock icon beside Settings gear in header
description: Add a small admin toggle icon (🔐/🔓) directly in the header, next to the Settings gear. Clicking it prompts for the admin password (or unlocks if already in admin mode) without navigating away from the current tab. Today the only way to go admin is via the Settings menu — you have to open Settings, authenticate, then navigate back. The new icon would let you unlock admin from anywhere (Drive tab, Audio tab, Choreo, etc.) in one click, with the same password modal already used elsewhere. Icon should reflect current state: locked (🔐) vs unlocked (🔓 or highlighted). No change to the Settings gear behavior.

title: AstroPixels+: animations custom — investiguer protocole + éditeur
description:
Firmware source: J:/R2-D2_Build/software/others/AstroPixelsPlus-main/

DÉCOUVERTE CLÉ: 23+ animations disponibles via commande @APLE{n} — notre driver utilise seulement @0T{n} (8 T-codes). RAINBOW(10), FIRE(22), etc. sont dans le firmware, juste pas exposés.

Mapping complet @APLE:
00=Normal 01=Alarm 02=Failure 03=Leia 04=March 05=SolidColor 06=FlashColor
07=FlipFlopColor 08=FlipFlopAlt 09=ColorSwap 10=Rainbow 11=RedAlert
12=MicBright 13=MicRainbow 14=LightsOut 15=Text 16=TextScrollLeft
17=TextScrollRight 18=TextScrollUp 19=RoamingPixel 20=HorizontalScan
21=VerticalScan 22=Fire 23=PSIcolorWipe 99=Random
+ effets custom 100=Bitmap 101=Plasma 102=MetaBalls 103=Fractal 104=FadeAndScroll

Paramètres @APLE: séquence, couleur (10 choix), vitesse (0-9), durée (0-99s), texte.

Pas de contrôle pixel direct via série — animations custom nécessitent modification firmware + recompile. Firmware open source, architecture LogicEffect (voir effects/*.h).

TÂCHES:
1. (QUICK WIN) Exposer les 23+ animations dans astropixels.py via @APLE — ferme le backlog '17 séquences manquantes'
2. (MOYEN) Endpoint API pour contrôle paramétrique (vitesse/couleur/durée)
3. (LONG TERME) Si animations vraiment custom voulues: modifier firmware + nouvel effect header + OTA flash

title: JS: choreo poll timer (_pollTimer) keeps running after leaving choreo tab
description: In master/static/js/app.js, _startPolling() creates a setInterval at 500ms to poll /choreo/status. onTabSwitch() (line 459) calls choreoEditor.init() when entering the choreo tab, but does NOT call _stopPolling() when leaving it. The 500ms polling continues on all other tabs until a choreo finishes or the user returns. Fix: call choreoEditor.stop() (or expose _stopPolling) from onTabSwitch() when tabId !== 'choreo'.

title: Android: allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs=true broader than needed
description: MainActivity.kt sets webSettings.allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs = true and allowFileAccessFromFileURLs = true (lines ~89). For a Pi-only app loading a local IP, this is overly permissive — it allows JS on file:// URLs to read all other file:// URLs without same-origin restriction. Should be false since the app only navigates to http://IP:5000, not file:// origins.

title: local.cfg writes non-atomic — power cycle during write can corrupt config
description: All blueprints that persist settings to local.cfg use a read-modify-write pattern with plain open('w') (settings_bp._write_key, vesc_bp._save_vesc_cfg, servo_bp arms config, behavior_bp). This truncates the file then writes the new content. If the process crashes, the Pi loses power, or the SD card has a write error between truncate and close(), local.cfg is left partially written or empty. On next boot, config_loader.py silently falls back to main.cfg defaults for all keys — robot boots with factory settings (power_scale=1.0, no arms config, no behavior settings, etc.) with no error shown to the user.
notes: Files: master/api/settings_bp.py:66-73 (_write_key), master/api/vesc_bp.py:49-59 (_save_vesc_cfg), master/api/servo_bp.py:398-411 (arms), master/api/behavior_bp.py:91-129. Fix direction: atomic write pattern — write to local.cfg.tmp then os.replace('local.cfg.tmp', 'local.cfg'). os.replace() is atomic on POSIX (rename syscall). Optionally add f.flush() + os.fsync(f.fileno()) before close for SD card safety. A single shared _write_cfg_atomic(path, cfg) helper in config_loader.py would fix all call sites at once.

title: Servo angles cached at setup() — calibration changes require service restart
description: dome_servo_driver.py loads dome_angles.json once at setup() into self._angles (line 128). After that, the cache is never invalidated. When the user saves new calibration via POST /servo/settings, servo_bp writes the JSON to disk and SCPs to Slave — but the running driver still holds the old angles in memory. The new values only take effect after a full service restart. Same issue applies to body_servo_driver on the Slave.
notes: Files: master/drivers/dome_servo_driver.py:99-128 (_load_dome_angles, called once at setup). master/api/servo_bp.py:141-186 (writes JSON but does not notify driver). Fix direction: add a reload() method to DomeServoDriver and BodyServoDriver that re-reads the JSON file and updates self._angles. servo_bp should call reg.dome_servo.reload() and notify Slave to reload body servos (new UART command SRV:RELOAD or direct driver call) after writing the JSON files. Alternatively, make _get_angle() read from disk each time (simpler but adds I/O on every servo command).

title: Audio Popen callbacks block UART thread — potential watchdog race
description: In slave/uart_listener.py, all UART callbacks are dispatched synchronously inline in the uart-slave-rx thread (_process_line:192). The audio driver's _launch() calls subprocess.Popen() inside a threading.Lock while holding the UART thread. If 6 audio channels are launched in rapid succession (e.g. dense choreo start), the H: heartbeat frame stays buffered in the OS serial buffer and is not processed until all Popen() calls return. Since watchdog.feed() is registered as a callback for H: (slave/main.py:168), both the ACK and the watchdog feed are delayed by the stacked Popen latency. Timing analysis: Popen on Pi 4B = 5-80ms per call. 6 calls x 80ms = 480ms blocking. Watchdog timeout = 500ms. Heartbeat interval = 200ms. Worst case total delay = 680ms > 500ms — watchdog fires and cuts VESCs mid-choreo. Risk is low under normal load (Popen ~10ms x6 = 60ms, well within margin) but becomes a real race under heavy system load (6 mpg123 processes already running + VESC telemetry + choreo computation).
notes: Files involved: slave/uart_listener.py:120-196 (_read_loop, _process_line) — slave/drivers/audio_driver.py:208-226 (_launch, subprocess.Popen inside lock) — slave/watchdog.py:86-104 (_watch_loop) — slave/main.py:168 (watchdog.feed registered as H callback). Fix direction: add a queue.Queue + dedicated audio worker thread in AudioDriver. UART callback posts (channel, path, volume) to the queue and returns immediately. Worker thread consumes queue and calls Popen. This decouples Popen latency from the UART thread entirely. Alternative (simpler but less clean): call watchdog.feed() hardcoded inline in _process_line before the callbacks loop, same as the H:OK ACK — this eliminates the feed delay regardless of callback duration.

title: Batterie: capteur courant ACS758 + ADS1115 sur Slave I2C
description: Monitorer le courant total batterie (6S 10000mAh XT90, pic ~45A). Solution: ACS758LCB-100B (Hall effect ±100A, conducteur passe dans le capteur) + ADS1115 (ADC 16-bit I2C 0x48) sur Slave. Exposer battery_current_a + battery_power_w dans health_stats, surface dans cockpit panel. PAS INA219 — trop petit (3.2A max).

title: RP2040: afficher IP Master au boot pour faciliter SSH
description: Au démarrage du Slave, envoyer l'IP wlan1 du Master vers l'écran RP2040 GC9A01. Permet de trouver l'adresse SSH sans avoir accès au router. Backlog depuis CLAUDE.md.

title: Pre-built OS images for Master and Slave Pis
description: Instead of running setup scripts, provide ready-to-flash .img files for Master and Slave. User flashes SD card, boots, done. Would include all deps, services, config templates pre-installed. Could be generated via pi-gen or a custom build script from this repo.

title: Web UI: QR code connexion rapide
description: Afficher un QR code pointant vers l'IP du Master pour connexion immédiate depuis n'importe quel téléphone. Pratique pour démos et conventions, donner contrôle temporaire sans chercher l'IP.

description: Currently bench_mode persists its last state (ON during dev phase). Before shipping/final assembly, change default to OFF in local.cfg and document that it should be disabled when VESCs are permanently mounted. Bench mode ON bypasses VESC safety lock — dangerous if forgotten in production.

title: Settings: Scheduler — séquences planifiées
description: Panneau Scheduler dans Settings: jusqu'à 5 slots heure+action (choreo ou son). Exemples: 14h00 → march.chor (parade convention), toutes les 15min → son random 'excited'. Toggle ON/OFF global. Thread scheduler dans main.py + persistance local.cfg [scheduler]. Complexité élevée. Priorité basse — fun mais pour plus tard. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-11-settings-menu-brainstorm.md

title: Script engine: commandes teeces,anim,N et teeces,raw,CMD
description: Ajouter dans script_engine.py les commandes teeces,anim,N (animation par numéro T-code) et teeces,raw,CMD (commande JawaLite brute). Complète le support lumières dans les séquences .scr. Design: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-24-light-editor-english-ui.md

title: Audio: niveaux de priorité avec éviction (high/normal/low)
description: Ajouter des niveaux de priorité aux slots audio. Règle: low/normal ne peuvent pas évincer high. Actuellement: preempt du plus ancien sans distinction. Metadata audio_channels_required dans .chor. Design: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-31-audio-multichannel.md

title: Sequences: recherche/filtre + favoris
description: Ajouter une barre de recherche dans le tab Sequences pour filtrer les choreos par nom. Système de favoris (étoile) pour accès rapide. Idée: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-12-sequences-tab-redesign.md

title: Backlog: AS5600 encoder magnétique position dôme
description: Intégrer un encodeur AS5600 I2C absolu magnétique pour la position du dôme (0-360° absolu, pas de dérive). Remplacerait le tracking odométrique actuel.

value: Choreo toolbar layout: Dropdown+NEW+DELETE on left group. chor-cmd-gap (margin: 0 20px = 41px) between DELETE and PLAY. Timecode+duration in chor-footer (26px bar at timeline bottom). Sequences tab: choreo_categories.json — 6 categories: performance/emotion/behavior/dome/test/newchoreo. Atomic write via .tmp + os.replace(). /choreo/list returns objects {name,label,category,emoji,duration} — backward-compat via n.name||n. ScriptEngine JS: _renderPills() + _renderGrid() + _attachCardEvents() + _attachDrag() + _startRename(). Loop mode: self._loop flag, reset t_start/ev_idx when t_now >= duration in _run(). Body servo inspector: when armsConfig._count > 0, arm servo IDs excluded from body_servos dropdown.

JS gotchas: (1) .hidden is NOT a global utility class — only defined for specific selectors, use style.display instead. (2) setPointerCapture in pointerdown breaks all child clicks — call only in pointermove AFTER 8px drag threshold. (3) --teal CSS var does not exist in :root (only --cyan) — always verify :root before using custom CSS vars. (4) choreoEditor._pollTimer (500ms /choreo/status): stopped via choreoEditor._stopPolling() in onTabSwitch() when tabId !== 'choreo' — without this poll runs in background on all tabs.

SERVOS/DÔME CALIBRATION (vérifié 2026-04-28): Fichiers: master/config/dome_angles.json (Servo_M0..M15), slave/config/servo_angles.json (Servo_S0..S15). Structure par servo: {label(max40chars), open(int 10-170°), close(int 10-170°), speed(int 1-10)}. Pas d'offset — angles absolus uniquement. Defaults hardcodés: open=110, close=20, speed=10 (dome_servo_driver.py:63-66). Chargement: une seule fois au setup() → cache self._angles, PAS relu à chaque commande. Fallback silencieux si clé manquante. Validation à l'écriture: _clamp() [10,170], _clamp_speed() [1,10], pas d'exception — silently clamped. SCP vers Slave: subprocess scp timeout=5s, artoo@r2-slave.local:/home/artoo/r2d2/slave/config/servo_angles.json (servo_bp.py:170-186). Déclencheur: POST /servo/settings.

UART RTT CALIBRATION ENDPOINT: GET /diagnostics/uart_rtt returns {count, min/avg/max/p50/p95/p99 in ms, current_body_uart_lat_ms, recommended_body_uart_lat_ms}. Recommendation = p50/2 rounded to 5ms, clamped [5..50]. UI in Settings → System → Hardware: MEASURE button + colored fit indicator (green/orange/red) + APPLY RECOMMENDATION (copies into input, user clicks SAVE to persist). p50 chosen over p95 because pyserial.read(timeout=0.1s) dominates high-percentile samples — p50 reflects steady-state physical hop.

ANDROID APP (vérifié android/app/src/main/ 2026-04-28): WebView wrapper Kotlin — PAS d'app native. Charge fichiers locaux bundlés: index.html (tablette >=600dp) ou mobile.html (mobile <600dp) depuis file:///android_asset/. Communication: REST HTTP uniquement, PAS de WebSockets. Heartbeat POST /heartbeat toutes les 200ms (mobile.js:30). Status poll GET /status toutes les 1000ms. Discovery: mDNS r2-master.local → IP sauvegardée → 192.168.4.1 → scan subnet 254 IPs en 50 threads. IP configurée dans SharedPreferences 'r2d2_prefs' clé 'host' (défaut 192.168.4.1). Port fixe 5000. Assets copiés depuis master/static/ — doivent être synchronisés manuellement si JS/CSS change.

Cockpit HAT diagnostic architecture: DomeServoDriver.hat_health() returns [{addr,ok,errors}] for Master-side servo HATs. Slave-side health (body servo HATs, motor HAT, RP2040 screen) flows via uart_health_server port 5001 -> reg.slave_uart_health -> status_bp /status. motor_hat_health is a one-shot I2C probe (read_byte_data addr 0x00) at each /uart_health request. JS SERVICES rows use data.master_location/data.slave_location from local.cfg [robot] not hardcoded. Falls back to generic row if arrays empty. display_ready+display_port from DisplayDriver.is_ready()/used_port.

local.cfg on Pi is gitignored — changes to local.cfg.example do NOT propagate to live Pi. Any default value change (e.g. repo_url) must be applied directly via SSH sed/paramiko after updating the example file. Pattern: ssh + sed -i on /home/artoo/r2d2/master/config/local.cfg

Settings sidebar: 12 panels — Bluetooth, Lock Mode, Arms, Calibration (ex-Servos), VESC, Lights, Battery, Audio, Camera, Network, Deploy, System. switchSettingsPanel(panelId) lazy-loads each panel. if (panelId === 'arms') armsConfig.load()

AUDIO CHANNELS CONFIG (vérifié settings_bp.py 2026-04-28): Endpoint: POST /settings/config avec {audio.channels: N}. Séquence complète: (1) write local.cfg [audio] audio_channels (2) lit slave.cfg local (3) write slave.cfg [audio] audio_channels (4) SCP slave.cfg → artoo@r2-slave.local:/home/artoo/r2d2/slave/config/slave.cfg (5) thread daemon sleep 2s → systemctl restart r2d2-slave ET r2d2-master. Slave lit au boot: slave/main.py:176 _read_audio_channels() → slave.cfg getint('audio','audio_channels',fallback=6). IMPACT: tous les sons en cours sont coupés au restart. Default=6, range 1-12 (local.cfg), hardcoded fallback=6. JAMAIS changer audio_channels sans accepter un restart complet des deux services.

Admin inactivity tracking: all tabs tracked via _activeTabId set in onTabSwitch(). Uses pointerdown not click — choreo editor blocks click via preventDefault(). Choreo Admin Guard: Save/Delete/Export/Import protected by password. New/Play/Stop/Load free. _choreoUnlocked flag: set true after first auth in CHOREO tab, reset false on tab exit. adminGuard._pendingCallback stored in showModal(), executed in _unlock() after auth. choreoEditor.save({ requireAuth: false }) bypasses guard for internal auto-saves (e.g. play on modified file).

AstromechOS long-term vision: image builder tool that asks all setup questions (robot name, passwords, WiFi, hotspot SSID, Git repo URL for OTA updates), produces two .img files for Master and Slave. User flashes, connects UART, done. Only servo calibration remains via UI. Makes the platform truly plug-and-play for any astromech builder.

CHOREO DURATION MODEL (2026-05-09): Per-arm block (arm_servos track) has 3 independent fields panel_duration/delay/arm_duration → backend honors each. all_dome bulk + duration → all dome servos slew over duration ✅. all_body bulk + duration → all NON-arm body panels slew ✅ but arms (panels + extensions) STAY at calibrated speed ❌ (intentional — _launch_arm_sequences shared with manual UI buttons + safe-home Reset E-STOP that genuinely want calibrated speed). User workaround: drop separate arm_servos blocks with explicit per-segment durations.

ChoreoPlayer duration details: _calcTotalDuration() = max(last_event_end + 2.0, 5.0) for canvas/ruler (visual padding). _calcPlaybackDuration() = max(last_event_end + 0.1, 1.0) for meta.duration sent to Pi. Pi stops 100ms after last event. Arms auto-dispatch: _ARM_PANEL_DELAY=0.5s between panel open and arm extension (and arm close to panel close). _read_arm_panel_map() reads local.cfg [arms] at start of each _run(). threading.Timer non-blocking, daemon=True.

Mode IA R2-D2 — vision complète à développer après robot fonctionnel. 3 couches: (1) Perception: Vosk/Whisper micro local + OpenCV caméra visage/personne + détection sons. (2) Cerveau: extension BehaviorEngine — machine à états Idle/Interacting/Following/Performing + mémoire courte. (3) Réactions: audio contextuel + Teeces sync + choreos par événement + dôme tourne vers personne + suivi personne. Tout local sur Master Pi, zéro cloud. Commandes vocales configurables depuis UI. Priorité: robot fonctionnel d'abord, IA en phase 2.
App Android renommée 'Astromech Control' (strings.xml). APK compilé = android/compiled/AstroMech_Control.apk (plus R2-D2_Control.apk)

Settings menu brainstorm (2026-04-11) — ideas still pending: (1) Service status indicator showing Master/Slave/Camera service health. (2) Experimental features toggle (feature flags for beta features). (3) Connection timeout tweaks (heartbeat intervals). (4) WiFi watchdog Level 1/2 hierarchical reconnection (try home WiFi first, fall back to hotspot). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-11-settings-menu-brainstorm.md for full list.

ARCHITECTURE MATÉRIELLE VÉRIFIÉE (source code 2026-04-28): Master=Pi 4B (dome, 192.168.2.104), Slave=Pi 4B 2GB (body, 192.168.4.171). UART physique: /dev/ttyAMA0 @ 115200 baud sur les deux Pis (shared/uart_protocol.py BAUD_RATE=115200). Heartbeat Master→Slave toutes les 200ms (HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS). Watchdog Slave: timeout 500ms (WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS) → emergency_stop_vesc() + affiche SYSTEM_LOCKED sur RP2040.

BRANDING RULE (2026-05-08): 'R2-D2' as software/program name → always 'AstromechOS'. 'R2-D2' as robot name (physical robot) → keep. Hardware mechanic descriptions (diff drive, motors) → 'robot'. Service names (r2d2-master, r2d2-slave systemd units) intentionally not renamed — breaking change requiring coordinated Pi migration.

E-STOP SEMANTICS (master/api/status_bp.py): /system/estop FREEZES (cuts vesc/dome/choreo, NO servo movement — bras/panneaux gardent leur position). /system/estop_reset stows at _SAFE_SLEW_SPEED=3 (slow, ~1s for 90°), reusing servo_bp helpers (_read_arms_cfg, _read_panels_cfg, _arm_servo_set, _panel_angle). Sequence: arms close (parallel) → wait per-arm delay → close panel → close non-arm body servos (parallel) → close dome servos (parallel). Each servo wrapped in try/except so one failure doesn't abort.

ENGLISH CODE STANDARD — when a French comment/docstring line is MODIFIED (not just read), the whole block it belongs to must be translated to English. Untouched French in unchanged files is acceptable. Applies to all Python files including slave drivers.

I2C BUS (vérifié source): Master PCA9685 @ 0x40 = 16 servos dôme (master/drivers/dome_servo_driver.py:57). Slave PCA9685 @ 0x41 = 16 servos body (slave/drivers/body_servo_driver.py:56). Les deux sur I2C bus 1. Pulse: 500µs + (angle/180)*2000µs.

VESC HARDWARE (vérifié slave/drivers/vesc_driver.py:88-89): VESC_ID_USB=1 (Flipsky Mini V6.7 connecté Pi Slave via USB, ttyACM auto-detect dans [ttyACM0,ttyACM1,ttyACM2], skip le port du RP2040). VESC_ID_CAN=2 (atteint via COMM_FORWARD_CAN à travers VESC1). MAX_ERPM=50000 par défaut. HARDWARE_SPEED_LIMIT=0.85. Télémétrie TL:/TR: toutes les 200ms. ATTENTION: 'Multiple ESC over CAN' DOIT être DÉSACTIVÉ sur les deux VESCs.

VESC PROPULSION CONFIG (vérifié 2026-04-28): MAX_ERPM=50000 défini slave/drivers/vesc_driver.py:94 — PAS modifiable UI, seulement UART VCFG:erpm:<N> (min 1000, pas persisté). HARDWARE_SPEED_LIMIT=0.85 défini vesc_driver.py:97 — hardcodé, pas configurable. Chaîne calcul ERPM: input[-1..1] × power_scale → clamp HARDWARE_SPEED_LIMIT → × max_erpm → VESC. power_scale: défaut 1.0 (registry.py:64), lu au boot depuis local.cfg [vesc] power_scale (main.py:217), UART VCFG:scale + local.cfg à chaque changement UI. Freinage/rampe: master/safe_stop.py — RAMP_MAX_MS=400, RAMP_STEP_MS=20, DEADZONE=0.08 — software-side uniquement, pas firmware VESC. Seuils choreo: vesc_max_current=30A, vesc_max_temp=80°C, vesc_min_voltage=cells×3.5V (choreo_player.py:135-145) — local.cfg [choreo] seulement, PAS d'UI. Modifiable UI: power_scale, invert_L/R (persistés), duty_mode (volatile). Non modifiable UI: MAX_ERPM, HARDWARE_SPEED_LIMIT, seuils de courant/temp.

E-STOP SERVO FREEZE PATTERN (2026-05-08): both DomeServoDriver (Master) and BodyServoDriver (Slave) expose self._frozen flag. _move_ramp checks at entry AND on every step → in-flight ramps abort, new SRV rejected. PWM stays at last commanded angle = full torque hold (NOT shutdown() which sleeps PCA9685 = drooping). New UART msg FREEZE:1/0 for Slave. system_estop sets freeze BEFORE choreo.stop() to unblock open_all().join(). system_estop_reset unfreezes BEFORE running safe-home. BT _trigger_estop also uses freeze() (was shutdown()). Frontend emergencyStop() must NEVER send /servo/*/close_all — they race the freeze and panels close instead of holding.

RP2040 manual flash: python3 -m mpremote connect /dev/ttyACM1 rm :display.py && python3 -m mpremote connect /dev/ttyACM1 cp /home/artoo/r2d2/rp2040/firmware/display.py :display.py. ALWAYS rm before cp — mpremote compares timestamps not content. VERSION file: update.sh always writes git hash — if stale, RP2040 shows SYNC FAILED.

AUDIO SLAVE (vérifié slave/drivers/audio_driver.py): déclenchement via UART commandes S: depuis le Master. Format: S:filename:CRC (channel 0), S:RANDOM:category:CRC, S:STOP:CRC, S2:/S3: etc pour autres channels. Backend: mpg123 via subprocess. Sons MP3 dans /home/artoo/r2d2/slave/sounds/ (gitignorés, 317 fichiers). Volume ALSA: amixer -c 0 cset numid=1 <vol>%. Channels audio: 6 par défaut (slave/config/slave.cfg).

BLUETOOTH GAMEPAD (vérifié master/drivers/bt_controller_driver.py 2026-04-28): Evdev-based, ~650 lignes. Boutons: BTN_EAST(305)=audio RANDOM:happy, BTN_WEST(304)=dome servo open/close, BTN_NORTH(307)=body servo open/close, BTN_MODE(316)=E-Stop (toujours actif). Axes: ABS_Y(1)=throttle (Y inversé: up=avant), ABS_X(0)=steer, ABS_RX(3)=dome. Deadzone=0.10. Differential drive: left=throttle+steer, right=throttle-steer*0.55 (amortissement 55%). Kids mode: multiplie par kids_speed_limit. Child Lock (lock_mode=2): bloque toute propulsion. Keep-alive thread: renvoie dernier (left,right) toutes les 300ms si _drive_active et |val|>0.01 (évite motion watchdog 800ms). Auto-reconnect: scan evdev toutes les 5s. Pairing via REST POST /bt/pair → bluetoothctl subprocess. bt_config.json n'existe pas encore — utilise defaults hardcodés.

CHOREO HOT-SWAP CONFIG (master/choreo_player.py): set_body_uart_lat(seconds) and set_audio_startup_lat(seconds) update self._* members atomically. settings_bp.set_config() calls these when 'choreo.body_servo_uart_lat' or 'choreo.audio_startup_lat' keys are written. New value applied at next play() OR next loop iteration (not mid-sequence to avoid timing jumps). Master HAT addresses are NOT hot-swappable (DOME_SERVOS is computed at import in servo_bp.py:109).

PROPAGATION CONFIG → DRIVERS (vérifié master/api/): Pattern A — write local.cfg direct (settings_bp, vesc_bp, behavior_bp). Pattern B — write JSON + SCP vers Slave (servo_bp: dome_angles.json local, servo_angles.json via SCP). Pattern C — registry only, pas persisté (ex: vesc duty mode). Pattern D — hot-reload avec swap atomique (lights backend: shutdown old driver, load new, swap reg.teeces). Pattern E — write + SCP + systemctl restart (audio channels: redémarre r2d2-slave via subprocess 2s délai). Pattern F — write slave.cfg + SCP + restart Slave only (slave HAT config: slave_hats, slave_motor_hat via _sync_slave_hat_cfg() dans settings_bp). Power scale et invert VESC: UART immédiat (VCFG/VINV) + registry, PAS de restart. Servo angles: lazy load (driver re-lit JSON au prochain appel).

SAFE-HOME DOME LIST FIX (2026-05-08): _safe_home() in status_bp had silent ImportError — 'from dome_servo_driver import SERVO_MAP' caught by bare except → dome_names=[] → step 4 ran zero iterations. Fix: use DOME_SERVOS from servo_bp (same source as body step 3 + UI). dome_servo_driver._servo_map is INSTANCE attribute, computed from configured HAT addresses. Lesson: never bare except on imports — use specific ImportError catch with explicit log.

UART SHUTDOWN command (2026-05-08): ajout SHUTDOWN:1:CRC → Slave fait sudo shutdown -h now après 3s délai (thread daemon, même pattern que REBOOT). Callback enregistré dans slave/main.py handle_shutdown(). Endpoint Master: POST /system/shutdown_slave (UART send) + /system/shutdown_both (UART send + Master shutdown 1s délai). Protocole UART complet: H=heartbeat, M=drive, D=dome motor, S=audio, DISP=display, VCFG/VINV=vesc config, TL/TR=telemetry, REBOOT=reboot slave, SHUTDOWN=shutdown slave.

NEVER hardcode installation-specific values (IPs, hostnames, paths, credentials) in Python code. Always read from local.cfg. The pattern: configparser.ConfigParser().read([MAIN_CFG, LOCAL_CFG]).get('section', 'key', fallback='default'). Offenders caught: servo_bp hardcoded 192.168.4.171 instead of reading local.cfg [slave] host. Reference files that already do it right: settings_bp.py (_read_cfg), vesc_bp.py (_LOCAL_CFG), behavior_bp.py (_get_cfg). Default fallback should be the .local mDNS name (r2-slave.local) for portability, NOT a fixed IP.

RP2040 DISPLAY (vérifié slave/drivers/display_driver.py): port auto-détecté parmi ttyACM0/ttyACM1/ttyACM2 — le premier disponible est pris par RP2040, les suivants par les VESCs. GESTION PRIORITÉS MULTI-CANAL (vérifié registry.py + bt_controller_driver.py 2026-04-28): Trois canaux concurrents: Web/Android (REST Flask), Gamepad BT (evdev direct), ChoreoPlayer (timer thread). Mécanisme priorité Web > BT: reg.web_last_drive_t et reg.web_last_dome_t (timestamps monotoniques) mis à jour par motion_bp.py à chaque commande web. BT controller vérifie: si time()-web_last_drive_t < 0.5s → skip la commande gamepad. Fenêtre priorité web = 500ms. Pas de mutex ni queue — accès concurrent aux drivers directs. BT et Choreo peuvent se chevaucher (pas de lock entre eux). E-Stop (BTN_MODE) contourne toutes les priorités — traité avant tout check.

SERVICE WORKER VERSIONED CACHE: master/static/sw.js has 'astromech-__VERSION__' placeholder. Flask /static/sw.js handler in flask_app.py reads VERSION_FILE and substitutes the placeholder at request time. Cache-Control: no-cache forces SW re-fetch. SLAVE BOOT BANNER PROTOCOL: Slave sends BOOT:READY:CRC after uart.start() settles (slave/main.py via _send_boot_banner thread). Master registers BOOT callback (master/main.py on_slave_boot) that re-pushes VCFG:scale + VINV:L + VINV:R from reg.vesc_power_scale/vesc_invert_L/R. Prevents config drift after Slave reboot mid-session. Test: ssh artoo@r2-slave.local 'sudo systemctl restart astromech-slave' then journalctl -u astromech-master | grep -i 'VESC config resynced'.

UART CRC CORRECTION (vérifié shared/uart_protocol.py:66): formule réelle = (sum(payload_bytes) + len(payload_bytes)) % 256, PAS juste sum % 256. CLAUDE.md ligne 'def calc_crc' est inexact — le +len() est intentionnel pour détecter insertion de null bytes (slipring BREAK → 0x00). Format message: TYPE:VALUE:CS\n, CS = 2 hex majuscules. Exemple: build_msg('H','1') → 'H:1:XX\n'.

Feedback: après toute modification de code (surtout backend/API), tester moi-même en injectant des valeurs de test via curl/urllib/python contre le Master live (192.168.2.104:5000) avant de présenter le travail comme terminé. Ne PAS attendre que l'utilisateur teste. Why: l'utilisateur l'a demandé explicitement le 2026-05-13 après le fix B-1 — il préfère que je valide mon propre travail, ça donne confiance et évite les allers-retours. How to apply: construire une matrice de cas (happy path + edge cases + adversarial inputs) en Python urllib (pas requests — pas installé sur le PC dev), hit les endpoints sur le Master déployé, présenter un résumé pass/fail avant de passer à la tâche suivante. PERSISTANCE SETTINGS (vérifié config_loader.py + blueprints 2026-04-28): Priorité: local.cfg OVERRIDE main.cfg (ConfigParser.read() séquentiel, dernier gagne). Séquence boot master: load() cfg → logging → network monitor → git pull → Phase1 drivers (UART/lights/deploy) → load VESC registry depuis cfg (main.py:217-219) → Phase2 servos → Phase3 ScriptEngine/ChoreoPlayer → BehaviorEngine → Flask. Pattern write: read-modify-write fichier entier (PAS atomique — pas de rename/fsync, truncate+write direct). Pas de cache write-through — chaque blueprint relit le fichier. Survie reboot: VESC scale/invert → local.cfg [vesc] ✓ | Servo angles → JSON files ✓ | Audio channels → slave.cfg via SCP ✓ | Behavior → local.cfg [behavior] ✓ | VESC duty_mode → registry volatile, PERDU au reboot ✗. Risque: écriture non-atomique de local.cfg — crash mid-write = fichier corrompu possible (rare mais non protégé).

Choreo timeline scroll fix (2026-05-13): when stacking many blocks pushed lower lanes off-screen, the 3 previous CSS fixes (min-height:0 on chor-body, align-items:flex-start on chor-tracks-body, overflow-x:clip on chor-scroll-wrap) all addressed inner flex chain layers but missed the root cause: #tab-choreo had NO height rule at all (unlike #tab-editor.active which has height:calc(100vh - var(--topbar-h) - var(--tabbar-h))). Without a viewport-bounded ancestor, .chor-layout's height:100% degraded to auto and overflow-y:auto on .chor-tracks-body never fired. Fix in commit c95c521: add #tab-choreo.active{display:flex;flex-direction:column;height:calc(100vh - var(--topbar-h) - var(--tabbar-h));overflow:hidden} AND change .chor-layout from height:100% to flex:1+min-height:0 (cohabits cleanly with #chor-audio-banner sibling). Lesson: when overflow:auto refuses to fire, walk UP the ancestor chain — the missing height bound is often at the tab/page container, not inside the broken-looking flex chain.

Tous les sliders custom (holo-slider et volume-slider) utilisent var(--val) pour le gradient CSS. Doit être sync via syncHoloSlider(element) : 1) dans oninput du HTML, 2) après toute assignation programmatique de .value en JS. Sans ça le thumb et la barre se désynchronisent.

Camera zombie stream fix: r2d2-camera.service uses Restart=always. scripts/camera-start.sh launches mjpg_streamer in background + 5s watchdog loop that kills the process if /dev/videoN disappears. Without this, the input thread dies silently on select() timeout but the main process stays alive as a zombie (no restart triggered). Service file updated on each deploy via sudo tee. Fake-hwclock: Pi 4B has no RTC — at boot the clock starts from the last fake-hwclock saved time (often weeks ago). systemctl status may show '2 weeks ago' for a service that just started this morning — normal, NTP corrects it after network connects.
